views.name: RulesTreeView
capabilities: Display hierarchical rules tree grouped by directory
Show lock status indicators on nodes
Display node detail panel (file, scope_glob, priority, hash, deps)
Select individual tree node to inspect
user_actions: Type in filter input to search nodes
Click a tree node to select and view detail
Click refresh button to reload rules + locks

views.name: HumanLockView
capabilities: Display all human-lock entries with drift status
Filter locks by all / drift / approved
Approve a drifted lock — writes hash update to server
user_actions: Click filter chips (all / drift / approved) to narrow list
Click approve button on a LockCard to call POST /api/human-lock/approve

views.name: IntentTimelineView
capabilities: Display ledger entries in dual-column AI / human timeline
Add text annotation to an AI ledger entry
user_actions: Click filter buttons to change source view
Submit annotation text on a TimelineEntry to call POST /api/intent/annotate

views.name: HistoryReplayView
capabilities: Scrub through ledger history using a range slider
View point-in-time rules tree snapshot for any ledger entry
Jump to latest entry with a button
Display replay metadata (mode, commit, replayed count)
user_actions: Drag slider to select ledger time point (triggers GET /api/history/state)
Click timeline list item to jump to that entry
Click 'Latest' button to seek to newest entry

views.name: DoctorView
capabilities: Display overall system health status (ok / warn / error)
Show summary cards: framework, protected paths, intent ledger age
List named diagnostic checks with per-check status and message
user_actions: Click refresh button to re-run GET /api/doctor

write_operations.effect: Updates the lock entry's accepted hash to the current file hash, clearing drift state
Creates a new human ledger entry linked to the referenced AI ledger entry ID, storing the annotation text

sse_subscriptions.update_mechanism: useEvents() hook in app.tsx opens a single SSE connection to /events via openSseConnection(). It maintains a RingBuffer of 50 events (fabricEventsSignal). On disconnect, exponential backoff reconnect triggers: initial 1s, multiplier 2x, cap 30s. lastEvent is passed as a prop to every view. Each view's useEffect watches lastEvent.type and calls its own load() when relevant event types arrive — pull-on-push pattern (SSE triggers a fresh GET, not direct state injection). Exception: IntentTimelineView's ledger:appended injects the payload directly into state without a refetch.

interaction_gaps.gap: No rule editing or creation UI
interaction_gaps.description: RulesTreeView is purely read/browse. Users can inspect rule metadata (file, scope, priority, hash, deps) but cannot edit, create, or delete any rule from the dashboard.
interaction_gaps.impact: Rule management must happen outside the dashboard via CLI or direct file edits, creating a context switch for operators.

interaction_gaps.gap: No bulk approve for human locks
interaction_gaps.description: HumanLockView requires approving each drifted lock individually. There is no 'approve all drifted' action.
interaction_gaps.impact: High friction when many files drift simultaneously, e.g. after a large refactor.

interaction_gaps.gap: Annotation is append-only with no edit or delete
interaction_gaps.description: IntentTimelineView can add a human annotation to an AI entry but there is no UI to edit or remove an existing annotation.
interaction_gaps.impact: Incorrect annotations are permanent from the dashboard's perspective.

interaction_gaps.gap: HistoryReplay is view-only — no rollback action
interaction_gaps.description: The history replay view lets users inspect past rule states but provides no mechanism to restore or roll back to a snapshot.
interaction_gaps.impact: Operators who identify a regression in rules cannot act from the dashboard; they must use CLI.

interaction_gaps.gap: Doctor view has no remediation actions
interaction_gaps.description: Diagnostic checks surface ok/warn/error status with messages but no clickable fix action exists for any check.
interaction_gaps.impact: Doctor is purely observational — all remediation happens outside the dashboard.

interaction_gaps.gap: No SSE reconnection visibility for the user
interaction_gaps.description: The header shows a 'connecting' badge during reconnect, but there is no indication of how many retries have occurred or what the next retry delay is.
interaction_gaps.impact: Users cannot distinguish a transient blip from a prolonged outage without browser dev tools.

read_write_ratio.summary: 3 of 5 views are functionally read-only (RulesTree offers filtering/selection but no mutations; HistoryReplay only queries; Doctor only queries). Only 2 views trigger server mutations: HumanLockView (approve lock) and IntentTimelineView (annotate entry). Total distinct write endpoints: 2 (POST /api/human-lock/approve, POST /api/intent/annotate). The dashboard is predominantly an observation and triage tool, with narrow but concrete write capabilities centered on human oversight actions.
